expected_trades_per_day_basis: Exit-side archetype — frequency is inherited from whichever entry it pairs with; cannot be computed standalone. In paper-shadow expect ~same trades/day as the paired entry (e.g. E126 ≈ 3443 trig / 30d ≈ 115/day raw, post-liquidity-filter likely 20-40/day on high-liquidity bucket).

best_in: Choppy/mean-reverting tape where price tags entry, drifts +0.3-0.8% then retraces — BE pulls stop to flat before the retrace eats the trade
High-liquidity majors (BTC/ETH/SOL/BNB) during US/EU overlap when 30s post-trigger price action is informative
Sessions with elevated realized vol but no strong directional trend (BE banks the wiggle, avoids the round-trip)
worst_in: Strong trending regimes where price moves linearly past +be_trigger_pct without retracing — BE clips winners early, capping right tail vs X100's looser floor
Low-liquidity / micro-cap symbols where the 30s post-trigger window is dominated by a single market-maker quote, not real flow
First 60s after a Reserved6 extreme-move alert — price often round-trips violently inside 30s, BE will flip to flat then reverse against you
regime_sensitivity.explanation: The 30s activation window is the load-bearing parameter: it assumes price discovery completes inside 30s on liquid pairs. On illiquid books or during news-driven extension moves that thesis breaks and BE either fires too early (clipping trends) or too late (after MFE has already round-tripped).

live_readiness_caveats: The liquidity_bucket=high filter MUST be enforced at execution time, not just backtest — if the live symbol universe drifts to include mid-caps the 30s BE assumption silently degrades and you will see live median trade % collapse vs paper.

be_trigger_pct and be_activation_seconds must both be swept in the experiment grid (synthesizer flagged this) — if only one is varied this archetype collapses onto X100 and the 7 keeps may be duplicates rather than a distinct edge.
BE-stop fills on Binance perp can slip 1-3 bps at the trigger price during fast moves; backtest assuming exact-price BE fills will overstate edge by ~0.05-0.10% per trade vs live.

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: <40% or >85% of trades activating BE in any 3-day window
monitoring_metrics.rationale: If <40%, the 30s/strict-trigger combo is too tight and this is just time_only in disguise; if >85%, BE is firing on noise and the archetype collapses to a near-zero-PnL flat-out machine.

Once BE arms, expected outcome should be small positive (winner runs) or exactly zero (BE fill). Negative median post-activation means slippage/latency is eating the BE protection — the core mechanism is broken.

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: >30% of trades that hit +1% MFE exit at exactly BE
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Direct measurement of the X100-vs-X105 tradeoff; if BE clips too many right-tail winners the strict 30s variant is strictly worse than the X100 floor.
This is the R2-E126 trap detector — paper showed +0.39 backtest → -13.5% on $1000. Track per-trade median divergence, not just cumulative PnL.
BE-style exits create runs of small flat/loss outcomes during trending regimes; 6+ consecutive signals the regime has shifted away from the chop-friendly assumption.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling Sharpe drops below 0 AND be_activation_rate >75% in same window
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: BE is firing constantly but not protecting PnL — the 30s window has stopped being informative on the current symbol/regime mix; edge is gone, not just noisy.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Live median trade % flips negative for >3 consecutive days while paper-shadow on same signals stays positive
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Pure execution-side decay (latency, slippage at BE trigger) — the archetype works on paper but cannot be realized live, same failure mode as R2-E126.
Strict 30s BE is provably destroying more right-tail than it saves left-tail — switch to X100 looser floor or kill the family.

expected_max_dd_basis: Breakeven family R3 stats: mean -0.0956, median -0.0805, max -0.08 (n=1923, 0 positive) — every backtested BE variant lost money at the cohort level. Strict 30s should be modestly better than the family median by clipping some left tail, but realistic paper DD on $1000 with 5-10% sizing over a 30-day window is 6-10%, anchored to the R2-E126 -13.5% precedent and the family's uniformly negative distribution.
compare_to_existing: X100 (breakeven floor baseline) is the direct sibling — X105 is X100 with stricter trigger + 30s activation gate + high-liquidity filter. For triangulation also compare to E126 paper-shadow (the R2 -13.5% trap): if X105 paired with E126 still produces backtest >0.3 but the new mean/kelly_capped/p25_capped_tail metrics flag it, that's the exact failure mode this archetype must NOT reproduce.
